marketContext.conflictAlert: FRAGILE CEASEFIRE — 60-day US-Iran MOU signed June 18, already tested June 22

conflictInsights.label: Defense backlogs are irrevocable
conflictInsights.desc: Multi-year RTX, LMT, NOC contracts signed during the active conflict cannot be cancelled — backlog strength persists regardless of ceasefire outcome

conflictInsights.label: Oil direction is still binary
conflictInsights.desc: WTI spiked to $84–88 during active conflict, then fell to ~$70.56 on the June 18 US-Iran ceasefire MOU — but a June 22 Trump strike threat already cracked the truce once. A re-escalation returns oil to $95–100+; a durable deal keeps it near $65–72

conflictInsights.label: Gold is losing to the rates trade, for now
conflictInsights.desc: Down 11% in June and 14% QTD to ~$4,020/oz as rising real yields overwhelm safe-haven demand — the structural central-bank buyer base is intact, but a hawkish Fed is the dominant near-term force

conflictInsights.label: NATO mandate is permanent
conflictInsights.desc: European 2% GDP defense commitment is now treaty-level — defense supercycle is structural, not tactical, and survives any Middle East resolution

themes.label: Fragile ceasefire
themes.desc: 60-day US-Iran MOU (June 18) reopening the Strait of Hormuz already cracked once on a June 22 strike threat — oil risk is live in both directions

themes.label: Fed turns hawkish
themes.desc: New Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC (June 17) flipped the dot plot to show 2026 rates ending higher, not lower — ~62% market odds of a September hike

themes.label: Fear despite records
themes.desc: CNN Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) even as the S&P and TSX hit record highs — a rare divergence signaling narrow, unconfirmed breadth

themes.label: AI capex intact
themes.desc: MSFT $120B+, AMZN $200B, GOOGL $175-185B, META $135B, NVDA ~$500B secured through 2026 — the one genuinely load-bearing bull case, and self-funding regardless of Fed path

themes.label: Healthcare secular
themes.desc: GLP-1 + aging demographics = multi-decade demand runway for NVO, ISRG, LLY regardless of macro cycle

themes.label: Inflation re-accelerating
themes.desc: May CPI hit 4.2% YoY (core 2.9%) on the Iran-war energy shock — the disinflation narrative from earlier in 2026 has stalled; June print due July 14

themes.label: TSX at records
themes.desc: Canadian equities at all-time highs on gold miners, Shopify, energy and financials; BoC held at 2.25% for a 4th straight meeting on weak domestic growth

themes.label: Copper at records
themes.desc: ~$6.19/lb, up 22.6% YoY on AI data-center and electrification demand — the cleanest corroborating signal for the capex theme

recommendations.name: Berkshire Hathaway B
recommendations.thesis: Sitting on $330B+ cash amid tariff volatility — Buffett deploys aggressively in corrections. Natural market hedge with zero dividend drag. Complements tech-heavy TFSA with value ballast. The ultimate shock absorber: it gets more valuable in downturns.
risks: Succession uncertainty post-Buffett
Underperforms growth in prolonged bull markets
Insurance exposure to catastrophic weather events

recommendations.name: Enbridge
recommendations.thesis: Largest North American pipeline — 30% of US crude flows through Enbridge's network. Revenue is set by the National Energy Board regardless of spot oil price: the safest energy income play in any environment. 6.8% dividend with 0% WHT in TFSA. 29 consecutive years of dividend growth. Own for income, not oil direction.
risks: Line 5 Michigan legal challenge — potential forced shutdown
Rate regulation caps revenue growth upside
High debt load (D/E > 1.5x) — rate-sensitive balance sheet

recommendations.name: Costco Wholesale
recommendations.thesis: Membership model is recession-proof — members don't cancel during downturns, they shop more. International expansion accelerating. Tariff pressure minimal as private-label pricing power offsets costs. One of the few retailers that benefits from consumer stress.
risks: Trades at 50x+ P/E — expensive for a retailer
Slowing international store openings vs expectations
Tariff pass-through compressing gross margins

recommendations.name: Visa
recommendations.thesis: Digital payments toll-road untouched by tariffs. Global cross-border volume growing as travel rebounds. Asset-light royalty model: $0.001 per transaction × billions of transactions = immense free cash flow. One of the widest moats in public markets.
risks: Regulatory interchange fee caps (EU, US)
Crypto payment rails as long-term substitute
DOJ antitrust scrutiny of card network duopoly

recommendations.name: Intuitive Surgical
recommendations.thesis: 7,000+ da Vinci robotic systems installed creates razor/blade recurring revenue. New Ion bronchoscopy expanding TAM. Secular shift to minimally invasive surgery = 20-year compounding runway. Hospitals don't switch once trained on a platform.
risks: Premium valuation (55x+ P/E)
J&J Ottava robotic system entering market
Procedure volume tied to elective surgery cycles

recommendations.name: Novo Nordisk
recommendations.thesis: GLP-1 peer to LLY at a cheaper valuation. Ozempic/Wegovy demand still exceeds supply. Oral semaglutide + next-gen CagriSema data due in 2026. Portfolio: LLY + NVO = dual GLP-1 coverage across two IP estates.
risks: CagriSema Phase 3 trial miss risk
LLY oral tirzepatide as direct competitor
US drug pricing reform compressing GLP-1 margins

recommendations.name: ARM Holdings
recommendations.thesis: CPU architecture royalty on every advanced chip. Powers all Apple silicon, Nvidia data center CPUs, and AI edge devices. Scales to $100B revenue with zero manufacturing risk — the ultimate AI leverage play without semiconductor capex risk.
risks: RISC-V open-source architecture gaining traction
Customer concentration (Apple, Qualcomm, Nvidia)
SoftBank overhang on share price

recommendations.name: Axon Enterprise
recommendations.thesis: AI law enforcement platform (Taser + cameras + Evidence.com SaaS). Government contracts = recession-proof revenue. International expansion accelerating. Zero dividend = pure TFSA compounder. Agencies that adopt Axon rarely leave — hardware, software and evidence management are all locked in together.
risks: Rich valuation (80x+ P/E)
Political / regulatory backlash on police surveillance AI
Government contract concentration risk

recommendations.name: Shopify
recommendations.thesis: Canadian fintech/commerce platform powering 10%+ of US e-commerce. AI tools (Sidekick) driving merchant GMV growth. Freed from logistics. Expanding into B2B commerce globally. Zero dividend = pure tax-free TFSA growth. Only Canadian tech name in the portfolio.
risks: Growth premium valuation — punished severely on any miss
Amazon and TikTok Shop competing for same merchants
Merchant GMV exposed to consumer spending slowdown

recommendations.name: RTX Corporation
recommendations.thesis: The most diversified defense compounder: Patriot missile systems, Raytheon munitions, Pratt & Whitney F-35 engines, and commercial aero MRO. Backlog extends past 2031 — ceasefire signals don't cancel a single unit. NATO 2% GDP commitment is now treaty-level, making RTX a decade-long structural compounder.
risks: GTF engine recall costs still unresolved
Cost-plus contract margin pressure from inflation
Ceasefire could slow near-term order intake optically

recommendations.name: Suncor Energy
recommendations.thesis: Suncor's ~$45/bbl break-even is the lowest of any major oil sands producer — profitable at $65 oil. 25%+ share buyback since 2020. CAD-listed = 0% WHT in TFSA. Size this position to your conviction on oil staying above $75. The energy cyclical with the most downside protection.
risks: WTI to $70–75 on ceasefire squeezes margins materially
Oil sands carbon tax escalation in Canada
Pipeline capacity constraints limit export optionality

recommendations.name: ASML Holding
recommendations.thesis: ASML is the only company on Earth that manufactures Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines — the equipment that physically etches transistors at 5nm and below. Every advanced chip from NVIDIA, TSMC, Samsung, and Intel requires an ASML EUV machine. There is no second supplier, no workaround, and a 10-year lead that cannot be replicated. The AI chip buildout is a direct secular tailwind: more advanced nodes means more ASML orders. Backlog now exceeds €40B.
risks: US export restrictions limiting China sales — largest single customer at risk
Customer concentration: TSMC + Samsung + Intel = ~80% of revenue
Extreme capital requirements to build next-gen High-NA EUV system

recommendations.name: Johnson & Johnson
recommendations.thesis: Johnson & Johnson has raised its dividend for 62 consecutive years — one of only a handful of 'Dividend Kings' globally. Post-Kenvue spin-off, JNJ is a cleaner, higher-margin business spanning pharmaceutical blockbusters (Darzalex, Stelara successors) and MedTech (surgical robotics, orthopaedics). The 3.2% yield with zero WHT in RRSP makes it the ideal defensive income anchor. It is the closest individual stock equivalent to a defensive bond position.
risks: Talc litigation settlements compressing near-term free cash flow
Stelara biosimilar competition eroding the flagship immunology franchise through 2026
MedTech procedure volumes tied to elective surgery cycles

recommendations.name: Royal Bank of Canada
recommendations.thesis: Royal Bank is Canada's largest bank and the most globally diversified of the Big Six. The Canadian banking system is an effective oligopoly — five banks face no meaningful domestic competition, benefit from regulated mortgage market structures, and have 30+ year records of uninterrupted dividends. RY's 4.2% dividend held in a TFSA pays with zero US withholding tax and qualifies for the Canadian dividend tax credit. RBC's growing US wealth management and capital markets franchise provides non-Canadian earnings growth.
risks: Canadian housing correction — mortgage book is the single largest tail risk
BoC rate cycle — net interest margin compresses if BoC cuts aggressively
Capital markets revenue is highly cyclical and discretionary

recommendations.name: McDonald's Corporation
recommendations.thesis: McDonald's is a real estate and franchise royalty business disguised as a restaurant chain. It owns the land and buildings that 95% of its franchisees operate on, collecting rent and royalties regardless of food cost inflation or wage pressures. With 40,000+ locations across 100+ countries, it is one of the most geographically diversified consumer brands on the planet. The dividend has grown for 47 consecutive years. In a consumer slowdown, McDonald's benefits from trade-down as consumers substitute sit-down restaurants for fast food.
risks: Consumer spending slowdown reduces traffic and royalty revenue directly
Reputational risk from decentralised franchisee operations (food safety, labour disputes)
Currency headwinds on international revenue repatriation to USD

recommendations.name: Roundhill Memory ETF
recommendations.thesis: The first ETF built entirely around DRAM, HBM, and NAND memory suppliers — a direct, concentrated bet on the AI infrastructure buildout's most cyclically explosive layer. Holds Samsung (~25%), SK Hynix (~24%), and Micron (~24%) across just 15 positions, becoming the fastest ETF in history to reach $10B AUM (43 days after its April 2, 2026 launch) as HBM demand for AI accelerators outstripped supply. Near-zero 0.15% yield means WHT drag is immaterial — TFSA lets the (likely volatile) capital gains compound tax-free, which is the entire point of holding this rather than the underlying names individually.
risks: 73% of the fund sits in just three names (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron) — this is a concentrated 3-stock bet dressed as a diversified ETF
Memory chips are a notoriously boom-bust commodity cycle; the fund's 134% YTD run reflects an unusually strong up-cycle that has historically mean-reverted hard
Only 3 months of trading history at time of writing, and a 2x-leveraged sibling product (RAM) signals this is being marketed as a momentum vehicle, not a core holding — size accordingly
0.65% expense ratio is high relative to broad semiconductor index ETFs

recommendations.name: Microsoft Corporation
recommendations.thesis: Azure is the #2 cloud platform and the primary enterprise on-ramp for OpenAI's models via the Copilot suite embedded across Office, Windows, and GitHub. Enterprise switching costs are enormous — once a company's identity, email, and productivity stack run on Microsoft 365, replacing it means re-training the entire workforce. The OpenAI partnership gives Microsoft first-mover AI monetization without carrying all of the model-training capex risk alone.
risks: Azure growth deceleration would hit the stock hard given its premium multiple
OpenAI relationship could shift as OpenAI diversifies compute partners
Antitrust scrutiny of bundling Copilot/Teams with Office

recommendations.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing
recommendations.thesis: TSMC manufactures the overwhelming majority of the world's leading-edge chips — every AI accelerator from NVIDIA, every advanced Apple/Qualcomm SoC, and virtually all cutting-edge silicon runs through TSMC's fabs. No competitor (Samsung, Intel) has matched its yields at the leading node. The AI buildout is a direct volume tailwind regardless of which chip designer wins.
risks: Geopolitical risk — Taiwan/China tension is the single largest tail risk to the entire semiconductor supply chain
Enormous capex requirements to stay ahead at each new node
Customer concentration in a handful of large fabless designers

recommendations.name: Broadcom Inc.
recommendations.thesis: Broadcom designs the custom AI accelerator chips (XPUs) for hyperscalers like Google and Meta who want an alternative to NVIDIA's general-purpose GPUs at lower cost and power draw. Its networking silicon (Tomahawk, Jericho) is also the backbone connecting AI data-center racks together. The VMware acquisition adds a second, high-margin enterprise software leg to the business.
risks: Custom ASIC demand is concentrated in a small number of hyperscaler customers
Integration execution risk on the large VMware acquisition
Cyclical semiconductor demand outside the AI-driven segments

recommendations.name: Palantir Technologies
recommendations.thesis: Palantir's Foundry and AIP platforms are embedded deep inside US and allied defense, intelligence, and government workflows — the kind of mission-critical, security-cleared integration that is extremely hard to rip out. Commercial enterprise adoption is now scaling alongside the government book. Zero dividend keeps this a pure tax-free TFSA growth compounder.
risks: Very rich valuation leaves little room for a growth miss
Government contract concentration and budget-cycle dependence
Commercial segment still needs to prove durable margins at scale

recommendations.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
recommendations.thesis: Kratos builds low-cost jet drones, target systems, and hypersonics test vehicles for the Pentagon — the 'attritable' (expendable, cheap-to-replace) end of the drone warfare shift that NATO's structural defense buildout is funding. Smaller and more volatile than the primes, but a purer bet on the unmanned-systems trend within defense spending.
risks: Small-cap volatility — moves much harder than RTX/LMT on any single contract news
Program-level revenue lumpiness quarter to quarter
Execution risk scaling manufacturing for larger production contracts

recommendations.name: Lockheed Martin
recommendations.thesis: Lockheed builds the F-35 — the sole fifth-generation fighter in full-rate production across NATO and allied air forces — plus missile defense and space systems with essentially no domestic competitor at that scale. NATO's treaty-level 2% GDP defense commitment locks in a decade-plus order book independent of any single conflict's outcome. A steadily growing dividend on top of the growth backlog.
risks: F-35 program cost overruns draw periodic political scrutiny
Cost-plus contract margins compress under persistent inflation
Single-program (F-35) revenue concentration

recommendations.name: Northrop Grumman
recommendations.thesis: Northrop is the sole-source prime on the B-21 Raider stealth bomber and the Sentinel ICBM modernization program — two multi-decade strategic-deterrent contracts that don't get rebid. Space systems (missile warning satellites, national security launch) add a second structural growth leg tied to the same NATO/allied defense buildout underpinning RTX and LMT.
risks: B-21 and Sentinel are both fixed-price development programs with cost-overrun history
Ceasefire de-escalation can slow near-term order optics even though backlog is largely untouched
Government shutdown / budget continuing-resolution risk delays contract awards

recommendations.name: Canadian Natural Resources
recommendations.thesis: CNQ is Canada's largest and most diversified producer — oil sands, conventional heavy oil, natural gas, and offshore — giving it more flexibility to shift capital toward whichever stream is most profitable than a pure-play peer. Decades of reserve life reduces reinvestment risk relative to shale-heavy US producers. CAD-listed means 0% withholding tax on the dividend inside a TFSA.
risks: Oil price direction remains genuinely two-sided post-ceasefire — margins compress hard below $65 WTI
Oil sands carbon tax escalation in Canada
Heavy oil differential widening pressures realized pricing

recommendations.name: Chevron Corporation
recommendations.thesis: Chevron's integrated model — upstream production plus downstream refining — smooths the swings a pure E&P producer feels on every oil-price move. Permian Basin scale keeps break-evens low, and the balance sheet is conservative enough to keep raising the dividend through down-cycles. A 4% RRSP-held yield with 0% US withholding tax under the Canada-US treaty.
risks: WTI direction is still binary post-ceasefire — a durable deal keeps oil range-bound near $65-72
Refining margins are their own separate, volatile cycle
Energy transition policy risk to long-duration project economics

recommendations.name: Home Depot
recommendations.thesis: Home Depot and Lowe's effectively duopolize big-box home improvement in North America, with Home Depot's Pro-customer relationships (contractors, tradespeople) providing a stickier, higher-margin revenue base than DIY-only retailers. Aging US housing stock supports steady renovation demand independent of new-home construction cycles.
risks: High mortgage rates suppress large discretionary renovation projects
Consumer spending slowdown hits big-ticket DIY purchases first
Housing turnover at multi-decade lows reduces move-in renovation demand

recommendations.name: Nike Inc.
recommendations.thesis: Nike remains the largest global athletic brand by a wide margin, with a direct-to-consumer digital channel that captures higher margin than the wholesale-heavy model of most apparel peers. A multi-year turnaround under new leadership is rebuilding wholesale relationships and product innovation cadence after a rough post-pandemic stretch — the entry point is cheaper than it's been in years relative to the brand's staying power.
risks: Turnaround execution risk — market share losses to Hoka, On, and Adidas aren't fully reversed yet
China demand remains a swing factor for both revenue and margin
Tariff exposure on manufacturing concentrated in Vietnam/Indonesia

recommendations.name: Walmart Inc.
recommendations.thesis: Walmart's grocery-led scale and logistics network let it out-price and out-deliver most competitors, and it now benefits from consumer trade-down in a slowdown the same way Costco does. A fast-growing, high-margin advertising and marketplace business is layering on top of the low-margin core, quietly improving the earnings mix. Low dividend yield keeps the WHT drag inside a TFSA small.
risks: Grocery is structurally low-margin — execution has to stay near-flawless
E-commerce and advertising growth needs to keep offsetting core margin pressure
Labour cost inflation across its enormous workforce

recommendations.name: Procter & Gamble
recommendations.thesis: P&G owns a portfolio of category-leading household brands (Tide, Pampers, Gillette, Crest) with genuine pricing power — consumers rarely trade down on the products they trust for their kids or their skin. 68 consecutive years of dividend increases make it a Dividend King, and the RRSP shelters the yield from US withholding tax under treaty.
risks: Private-label and value-brand competition intensifies in a consumer pullback
Input cost inflation pressures margins between pricing cycles
Slow-growth, low-beta profile lags in strong bull markets

recommendations.name: TC Energy
recommendations.thesis: TC Energy operates one of North America's largest natural gas pipeline networks, with revenue largely locked in under long-term, regulated take-or-pay contracts — cash flow that barely moves with spot oil or gas prices. Post the South Bow spin-off, the remaining business is a purer, simpler regulated-pipeline story. CAD-listed for 0% withholding tax on a 5%+ yield inside a TFSA.
risks: High leverage typical of pipeline operators — rate-sensitive balance sheet
Permitting and regulatory delays on any new build-out capacity
Natural gas demand growth assumptions tied to LNG export buildout

recommendations.name: Kinder Morgan
recommendations.thesis: Kinder Morgan operates one of the largest natural gas pipeline networks in the US, moving roughly 40% of the natural gas consumed domestically. Like the Canadian pipelines, the bulk of cash flow sits under long-term contracts largely insulated from commodity price swings — and rising LNG export and AI-data-center gas demand both run directly through its footprint. RRSP shelters the yield at 0% US withholding tax under treaty.
risks: High leverage typical of the midstream business model
New pipeline permitting faces persistent regulatory and legal challenges
Interest-rate sensitivity given the debt-funded growth model

recommendations.name: iShares Core S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index ETF
recommendations.thesis: The lowest-cost, broadest way to own the Canadian market in one ticker — 200+ TSX names spanning banks, energy, materials, and railways, capped so no single mega-cap dominates the fund. Because it holds only Canadian stocks, there's no cross-border withholding tax drag in a TFSA, making it a clean core holding to anchor a portfolio around individual stock picks rather than a replacement for them.
risks: Heavy sector concentration in Financials and Energy versus a truly global index
Canada-specific macro risk (housing, oil, single-country currency exposure)
0.06% MER is low but the fund still can't beat the index it tracks

recommendations.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
recommendations.thesis: One ticker for 500 of the largest US companies at a 0.03% expense ratio — the default core holding most professional allocators would choose if forced to hold just one thing. US-listed ETFs held in an RRSP are treaty-exempt from US withholding tax on distributions, which is not true in a TFSA, making the RRSP the structurally correct account for this exact fund.
risks: Market-cap weighting means the fund is increasingly concentrated in a handful of mega-cap tech names
No valuation discipline — buys more of whatever has already gone up
US-dollar exposure adds a currency variable for CAD-based investors
